title: eriogonum baileyi var. davidsonii
intro: Eriogonum baileyi var. davidsonii, commonly known as Davidson's buckwheat or Davidson's desert buckwheat, is a variety of flowering plant in the buckwheat family that is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Davidson's desert buckwheat include its ability to support skin health, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb for topical use. Davidson's desert buckwheat can be prepared through herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of skin issues and promote overall skin health.
